question_text: On which of the following planets would an object with a mass of 10 kg have the greatest weight?

options.label: A
options.text: The Moon, where gravitational field strength is approximately 1.6 N/kg

options.label: B
options.text: Earth, where gravitational field strength is approximately 10 N/kg

options.label: C
options.text: Jupiter, where gravitational field strength is approximately 25 N/kg

options.label: D
options.text: Mercury, where gravitational field strength is approximately 3.7 N/kg

correct_answer: Jupiter, where gravitational field strength is approximately 25 N/kg
explanation: Weight is calculated using the formula: Weight = mass × gravitational field strength. Since the mass is constant at 10 kg, the object will have the greatest weight where the gravitational field strength is greatest. Jupiter has a gravitational field strength of 25 N/kg, which is stronger than the Moon (1.6 N/kg), Earth (10 N/kg), or Mercury (3.7 N/kg), so the object would weigh most on Jupiter.
